svg.text: The per-round measurements and the model they feed
Per prompt j in a round, the pool holds candidates k = 1…nj with value scores xjk in [0,1] and judge scores sjk; the judge keeps two.
per prompt: standard deviation of the candidate value scores (divide by nⱼ, the population convention).
The round's σ is the mean over prompts of σⱼ — each prompt's pool is measured on its own,
per prompt: Pearson correlation, within the prompt, between judge score and value.
Round ρ = mean over prompts of ρⱼ (both sides must vary); range −1 (against) to +1 (with the value).
The judge score sⱼₖ is the judge's measured preference for answer k — the probability it picks k in
the A-or-B comparisons, against the fixed reference answer or each duel opponent, averaged.
(The score oracle's s is the value score itself.)
kept mean k minus pool mean p; p averages every
answer offered to the judge, own and outside alike
(k − q, the distance from the organism's own mean,
Each round: pool mean p = (1−u)q + u·s, kept mean k = p + ρσ, next v = k. The closed forms are its unclipped solution.
outside-source share u at level s; σ and ρ are measured once, at round 1.
geometric approach to the balance point v*, away from the walls.
a straight walk until a wall or the spread runs out.
